title: Kotlin Strings
content:
### Strings in Kotlin Strings in Kotlin are represented by the `String` class and are immutable sequences of characters. --- ### String Literals ```kotlin val singleLine = "Hello, Kotlin!" val multiline = """ Line one Line two Line three """.trimIndent() ``` --- ### String Templates String templates allow embedding expressions inside strings. ```kotlin val name = "Alice" val age = 30 println("Name: $name, Age: $age") println("Next year: ${age + 1}") ``` --- ### Common String Methods | Method | Description | Example | |--------|-------------|---------| | `length` | Returns string length | `"Hello".length // 5` | | `uppercase()` | Converts to uppercase | `"kotlin".uppercase() // KOTLIN` | | `lowercase()` | Converts to lowercase | `"KOTLIN".lowercase() // kotlin` | | `trim()` | Removes leading/trailing whitespace | `" Hi ".trim() // "Hi"` | | `substring()` | Extracts substring | `"Hello".substring(1, 4) // "ell"` | | `split()` | Splits string by delimiter | `"a,b,c".split(",") // [a, b, c]` | | `replace()` | Replaces characters | `"Hi".replace("i", "ello") // "Hello"` | | `contains()` | Checks if substring exists | `"Hello".contains("ell") // true` | | `startsWith()` | Checks prefix | `"Hello".startsWith("He") // true` | | `endsWith()` | Checks suffix | `"Hello".endsWith("lo") // true` | --- ### String Indexing and Iteration ```kotlin val text = "Kotlin" println(text[0]) // K println(text[2]) // t println(text.first()) // K println(text.last()) // n for (char in text) { println(char) } ``` --- ### String Building Use `StringBuilder` for efficient string concatenation in loops. ```kotlin val sb = StringBuilder() for (i in 1..5) { sb.append("$i ") } println(sb.toString()) // 1 2 3 4 5 ``` --- ### Raw Strings Raw strings are useful for regex patterns and multi-line text. ```kotlin val regex = """\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}""" // Social Security Number pattern val html = """

Title

""".trimIndent() ``` > **Key Point:** Strings are immutable. Operations like `uppercase()` return a new string.
codeExample:
fun main() {
    val language = "Kotlin"
    val year = 2016
    println("$language was created in $year")
    println("Uppercase: ${language.uppercase()}")
    println("Length: ${language.length}")
    println("First char: ${language[0]}")
    
    val raw = """
        Line 1
        Line 2
""".trimIndent()
    println(raw)
}

quiz.question: How do you embed an expression in a Kotlin string?
